groupSummary: Numerous claims link ultra-processed foods to various health issues, including cancer and chronic diseases.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are linked to increased risk of several cancers, particularly those affecting the digestive system.
claims.postExcerpt: the growing body of scientific evidence paints a far more troubling picture of their impact on human health.

claims.claimText: New research links ultra-processed foods to 12 serious health conditions.
claims.postExcerpt: what this means, what to watch for, and how to make smarter food choices.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are linked to obesity, heart disease, diabetes, cancer, mental health disorders, and dementia.
claims.postExcerpt: we uncover the hidden dangers of ultra-processed food.

claims.claimText: Evidence shows that many preservatives in ultra-processed foods are tied to increased cancer risk.
claims.postExcerpt: Data from @bmj_latest showed many preservative additives...

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are linked to higher cancer risk, faster aging, and cognitive decline.
claims.postExcerpt: the hidden dangers of ultra-processed foods (UPFs)...

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are associated with chronic diseases and constant hunger.
claims.postExcerpt: linked to early death, chronic disease, and constant hunger.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods can disrupt our gut microbiome and increase our eating rate.
claims.postExcerpt: how these industrial food formulations are driving chronic disease worldwide.

groupTitle: Identification and Characteristics of Ultra-Processed Foods
groupSummary: Claims focus on how to identify ultra-processed foods and their characteristics.

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are significantly altered from their original form using industrial processes.
claims.postExcerpt: foods that have been significantly altered from their original form...

claims.claimText: Examples of ultra-processed foods include packaged snacks, sugary drinks, and ready-to-eat meals.
claims.postExcerpt: from breakfast cereals and biscuits to flavored drinks...

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods often contain artificial flavors, colors, preservatives, and additives.
claims.postExcerpt: loaded with artificial colors, preservatives, excess sugar...

claims.claimText: The NOVA classification system is used to identify ultra-processed foods.
claims.postExcerpt: the gold standard used by researchers worldwide...

claims.claimText: Many ultra-processed foods are marketed as healthy but are actually harmful.
50-70% of foods in supermarkets are ultra-processed.
claims.postExcerpt: Did you know that 50–70% of the foods in our supermarkets are ultra-processed?

groupSummary: Claims discuss the economic implications and societal trends related to ultra-processed foods.

claims.claimText: The rise of ultra-processed foods is linked to changes in lifestyle and family structure.
claims.postExcerpt: the growing dependence on packaged and ultra-processed foods...

claims.claimText: Ultra-processed foods are often more affordable than fresh, whole foods.
claims.postExcerpt: chemically processed and ultra-processed foods are typically high in added sugar...

claims.claimText: Aggressive marketing of ultra-processed foods contributes to their consumption.
claims.postExcerpt: how ultra-processed foods, factory farming, antibiotic overuse...

claims.claimText: The convenience of ultra-processed foods is driving their popularity despite health risks.
claims.postExcerpt: Ultra-processed foods: convenient, but at a cost.
